pr.title: Fix another bad JObj function name and bad functions.h declaration
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Small cleanup PR correcting a mislabeled JObj assembly symbol and a bad functions.h declaration. The function previously labeled HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s was renamed to JObjResetRST, and the internal branch target was updated accordingly. include/functions.h was kept in sync by replacing the commented unknown declaration with JObjResetRST and by demoting the HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub prototype back to a commented declaration with an HSD_JObj pointer type.

details: Changed the global label at address 8036ED3C from HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty to JObjResetRST.
Updated the branch at 8036EF20 to call JObjResetRST instead of HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty.
No instruction bytes changed in the excerpt; this was a symbol/name correction in the assembly source.
Changed the commented unknown declaration from HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty to JObjResetRST.
Changed HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub from an active prototype using void* to a commented prototype using struct _HSD_JObj*.

smart_moves: Kept assembly labels and header declarations aligned, avoiding a stale symbol name in functions.h after renaming the asm label.
Updated internal assembly callsites to use the corrected symbol name, making future cross-references point at the intended JObj reset function rather than a misleading matrix-dirty query name.

Avoided keeping an active HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub declaration when the available declaration was apparently not trusted; the PR comments it out and gives the more specific struct _HSD_JObj* type as a hint for later work.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation scaffolding, a wrong symbol name can be worse than an unknown func_* name because it misleads future matching and semantic analysis.
When correcting an asm label, update every local branch/call reference and the central declaration list in the same PR.
If a function prototype is uncertain, prefer leaving it commented with the best-known type rather than exposing a possibly wrong active declaration.
JObj-related names should be checked against behavior before applying HSD_ prefixes or descriptive names such as MtxIsDirty; nearby call relationships alone may not prove semantics.

naming_conventions.evidence: asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s global label and branch call were renamed from HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty to JObjResetRST.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub
naming_conventions.note: functions.h changed this from an active void* prototype to a commented struct _HSD_JObj* prototype, indicating the declaration was considered bad or not ready.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Pure label rename in assembly can improve symbol accuracy without changing instruction output.
Callsite symbol names in handwritten asm should be updated after a label correction so objdump/source navigation does not continue propagating the old semantic guess.
Header prototypes should not be prematurely enabled if the argument type or function status is uncertain; commented prototypes can preserve searchable knowledge without affecting compilation.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining references to HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty to ensure the bad name was fully removed.
Search for JObjResetRST versus HSD_JObjResetRST to clarify whether both names refer to distinct functions or whether one is still a naming inconsistency.
Revisit HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub once its signature is known well enough to restore an active prototype.
Check later PRs touching asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s for eventual decompilation of JObjResetRST or HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.
